comment: Both are dependent particulars (tropes) inhering in/dependent on a bearer, fully manifested whenever they exist. Not `exact-equivalence-candidate` because DOLCE additionally formalizes the Quale layer, which BFO's definition (as found in this pass) does not mirror explicitly.
Same trope-theoretic convergence as mapping-001; UFO's Quality additionally nests under the broader Moment category and the value-space criterion.

**Raised 2026-07-01** from medium: both pair a particular property instance with a value/space notion (Quale / value space, UFO even using the identical term "quale") — the closest structural match found in this crosswalk, and now confirmed to share a theoretical origin (`note-002`): both sources independently cite Gärdenfors' conceptual-spaces theory.

Explicitly not equivalent: BFO's Quality is a particular dependent on a bearer; SUMO's Attribute is an abstract entity. This is a confirmed metaphysical divergence, not an unresolved uncertainty — high confidence in the *non*-equivalence itself.
Same trope-theoretic convergence as `mapping-001`/`mapping-002`: both are dependent particulars inhering in/dependent on a bearer, joining the same camp as BFO/DOLCE/UFO rather than SUMO's abstracta.

Same trope-theoretic convergence; additionally, GFO's own `Value_space` explicitly cites DOLCE (Masolo, 2003) by name for the value-space/quality-space parallel (`note-005`), strengthening this beyond an editorial observation alone.

Same rationale as `mapping-004`/`mapping-005`: GFO's `Property` is a `Concrete`, `Dependent` individual (a particular), while SUMO's `Attribute` is an abstract entity — the same confirmed metaphysical divergence extended to a fourth source.

Both are dependent particulars tied to an individual entity ("a certain dependent property possessed by an entity"). `medium-high`, not `high`, specifically because YAMATO's own OWL/Hozo files are not fetched (quotation-only citation) — a genuine sourcing-completeness gap, not because of the elaboration difference alone: YAMATO's four-tier apparatus (generic type/role type/quality/instance) is more elaborated than BFO's single Quality class, but that structural fact is confidently known, not itself an open question.

Structurally the closest pairing among the new sources: both split a particular quality from its value/realization (DOLCE: Quality/Quale; YAMATO: Quality/Quality instance), and YAMATO's own text explicitly engages DOLCE's quality-space concept by name (`note-005`) rather than this crosswalk drawing the comparison unassisted. YAMATO explicitly argues DOLCE's coverage is incomplete — a disclosed point of disagreement, not a clean equivalence, but a confidently and directly quoted one (YAMATO's own critique), not an open sourcing question, so it does not itself justify a hedged confidence value.

No TUpper correspondence identified; TUpper reifies no Quality/Attribute/Property class, only direct measurement relations (`mass`, `length`, etc.) from object to value — a third modeling style, neither trope nor abstracta (`note-004`). Recorded explicitly rather than silently omitted, per this crosswalk's established practice for negative findings.

The only new source with a class literally named "Quality" (via subclass naming), but GUM's criterion is linguistic (adjectival realizability) rather than metaphysical inherence — a terminological match without a matching definitional criterion, the same pattern already found for GUM's Process in `process.md`.
